summary: Frontend regression for new product types (standard / unit_based / variant) across POS, Inventory and checkout. Seeded shop d5080217..., created TEST_Sugar Bulk (unit_based, base 50000g, 250g@40, 500g@75) and TEST_Sneakers (variant, Size 40 stock 5, Size 42 stock 3, Size 44 stock 0) via /api/products. Logged in as owner.seed and exercised: (1) POS product cards — UNITS badge + 'from KES 40' + '50.00 kg left' on Sugar Bulk; VARIANTS badge + 'from KES 2500' + '8 in stock' on Sneakers; standard Seed Phone shows 'KES 300' + '+ Add'. (2) Standard direct add to cart works. (3) Picker modal opens for unit_based, lists 250g/500g with subtitle and price, closes on pick. (4) Picker modal opens for variant, sold-out Size 44 button is disabled. (5) Cart adds two separate lines for 250g vs 500g vs Size 40, each with its own _lineId; the per-line + button increments only that line. (6) Cash checkout for a mixed cart (1× Seed Phone, 2× Sugar Bulk 250g, 1× Sugar Bulk 500g, 1× Sneakers Size 40) succeeded → receipt shown → backend stock atomically decremented as expected: Seed Phone stock 10→9, TEST_Sugar Bulk base_stock 50000→49000 (250×2+500=1000), TEST_Sneakers Size 40 5→4 with Size 42 & 44 untouched. (7) Inventory tab renders 'inventory-stock-{id}' as '📦 49.00 kg' for the unit-based product (no raw grams in parens), '📦 7 (3 variants)' for the variant product, '📦 9' for standard, with UNITS / VARIANTS badges via inventory-type-{id} for the new types and no badge for standard.

issues: Category renders as raw slug 'fashion' instead of '👜 Fashion' label. categoryLabel(p.category) returns the slug when not in DEFAULT_CATEGORIES; same on POS card. Minor but inconsistent with other categories.
Per-line + and - buttons render with no left padding so they sit very close to the price text. Functional but slightly cramped on narrow viewports.

action_items: Optional polish: in Inventory and POS cards resolve p.category through a label map that also covers DB-defined categories (categoryLabel currently only checks DEFAULT_CATEGORIES, so 'fashion' falls through). Not a blocker.
Optional UX: surface an inline 'Sold out' state in the cart if a unit-based line's qty * unit.quantity exceeds remaining base_stock_quantity — today the user only learns at checkout.
critical_code_review_comments: PosApp.jsx add(): for unit_based products stock is set to Infinity at the line level. This relies entirely on backend enforcement on checkout; if the checkout fails the user sees a generic alert(JSON.stringify(detail)). Consider mapping the backend error into a friendlier toast.

PosApp.jsx ProductPickerModal subtitle uses `${u.unit || product.base_unit}` but the create_product route currently strips the per-unit `unit` field (selling_units stored as {label,quantity,price} only — see API response). It works because base_unit is used as fallback, but the optional per-unit unit override is effectively dead code today.
PosApp.jsx is 1200 lines (POS shell + Receipt + ProductPickerModal); split ProductPickerModal and Receipt into their own files for readability and to enable unit testing.
POS card 'stockLabel' for variant uses simple sum of v.stock without subtracting v.reserved. Acceptable for hint, but slightly overstates available count when concurrent reservations exist.
ProductsPage.jsx renderStock for variant shows total stock across all variants but does not surface per-variant low-stock; consider a tooltip listing each variant when count is low.
seed_data_creation: Called POST /api/dev/seed, then created two TEST_-prefixed products via POST /api/products (multipart): TEST_Sugar Bulk (id 42689f9f-ee76-464e-853c-878b232985c1, unit_based, base_unit=g, base_stock_quantity=50000, selling_units 250g@40 and 500g@75) and TEST_Sneakers (id f11a0724-2562-40a7-bcdf-89a6426a952d, variant, Size 40 stock 5, Size 42 stock 3, Size 44 stock 0). After the test the products remain with decremented stocks (49000g sugar; Size 40 stock 4); rerunning the test will continue to deduct, so re-seed if needed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: POS route uses query param `shopId` (camelCase), not `shop_id`. Login form inputs are placeholder-based ('Email','Password'), not type=email. Owner panel sidebar has an 'Inventory' link; the inventory page has a top-right shop dropdown (the 2nd <select> on the page) that you must use to switch to the seeded shop. Backend stock decrement on POS checkout was verified against the public preview URL via direct GET /api/products. Public storefront /shop/dukani-store flow was NOT executed in this iteration — code in /app/frontend/src/pages/PublicShopPage.jsx mirrors the POS picker logic but should still be smoke-tested if requested.
rca of the issue: n/a — no defects found in this iteration.
